new_listings.title: Specialized M2 26” Stumpjumper 1998
new_listings.description:
Clearing out the garage, way too many bikes.  I have available a 1998 Specialized M2 Stumpjumper in original green. There are no dents on the 15” frame just some scratches through out from the years. The bike is solid and none of it affects the functionality of the bike! Own this piece of history with a few modern upgrades for a good solid ride! 

Frame: Stumpjumper M2 Metal Matrix 26”
Groupo: 8 speed Shimano XTR
-crankset: race face triple 
Fork: Fox 32 Performance Disk. 
Tires: Serfas HP 26x2.0

Asking $900 OBO

new_listings.title: Cannondale Habit Medium Size
new_listings.description:
This medium size cannondale is in great condition, this is my sons bike that he never really rode because we moved out of town, he got an e-bike and took the breaks off this bike to put on his e-bike so it does need a set of breaks and a 20 dollar fix, Overall fun bike in great condition.

- rear rechargeable tail light 
-front fender 
- new grips 
- new spike pedals 

Cons
-The derailer (hanger) is broken under 20 dollars to replace 
- no brakes
 
Serious buyers only

active_shortlist.title: Canyon Grand ON 7 E-Bike, Size - M, 2025 Full suspension carbon E-MTB
🚴‍♂️ Cannondale Habit Full Suspension Mountain Bike – Like New!
active_shortlist.description:
🚴‍♂️ Cannondale Habit Full Suspension Mountain Bike – Like New!

💰 Price: $1,500 OBO
📍 Location: Costa Mesa, CA
🧼 Condition: Ridden only twice – excellent, like new

⸻

⚙️ Specs & Components
	•	Frame: Large (fits riders 5’10”–6’2”) – lightweight aluminum Zero Pivot Construction
	•	Wheels: 27.5” (650b) with Schwalbe Nobby Nic tires – great tread
	•	Front Suspension: RockShox Recon Silver TK – 130mm travel
	•	Rear Shock: RockShox Monarch RT – 275 PSI max
	•	Crankset: FSA V-Drive 36/26T
	•	Drivetrain: Shimano 10-speed setup
	•	Brakes: Hydraulic disc brakes – strong stopping power
	•	Pedals: RaceFace Aeffect (blue anodized, alloy) – $120 upgrade
	•	Seat: Brand new Cannondale performance saddle
	•	Rims: WTB ST i23 Tubeless-Ready alloy
	•	Extras: Handlebar light + optional size 12 MTB shoes

⸻

🔹 Highlights

✅ Like new – only 2 rides total
✅ Smooth full suspension front & rear
✅ High-end upgrades (RaceFace pedals + new saddle)
✅ Stored indoors, no rust or wear
✅ Perfect for trail, enduro, or all-mountain riders

⸻

💬 Seller Notes

This bike rides incredibly smooth and was built for performance. It’s been meticulously cared for and barely used — a fantastic deal for anyone looking for a premium trail bike without paying new retail prices (originally ~$2,800+).

⸻

💰 Asking $1,500 OBO

Cash only accepted.
Pickup in Costa Mesa, CA

active_shortlist.title: Santa Cruz Hightower Carbon - S Kit - 2019
active_shortlist.description:
Santa Cruz Hightower Carbon - S Kit - 2019 with Reserve Carbon Wheels


Built for big days in big terrain.

The Hightower brings versatility and brawn to the game while hitting a sweet spot for aggressive trail riding. It sports 135mm of rear travel, fast-rolling 29er wheels, and a 67-degree head angle. 

The Hightower gives you the extra bit of confidence to let off the brakes and let 'er rip through the rough stuff. It feels well-balanced in the air and corners like a champ. At the same time, it is has impeccable trail manners and pedals efficiently, so you won’t get that ‘too much bike’ feeling if you end up doing an extra lap or pushing your ride further into the hills.

Santa Cruz Hightower

* 		135mm (5.3") VPP™ suspension
* 		148mm Rear Axle Spacing
* 		15mm thru-axle fork
* 		27.5+ or 29" wheels
* 		Bottle cage mount within front triangle fits piggyback shocks
* 		Carbon C and Carbon CC frame options
* 		Double sealed pivots for long bearing life
* 		Integrated Headset (IS41; IS52)
* 		Internal carbon tubes ensure precise and hassle-free routing of derailleur and seat post cables
* 		ISCG-05 tabs for chainguide compatibility
* 		Threaded Bottom Bracket


Specifications
Weight: 12.88kg / 28.4lbs
Material: Carbon C
Wheel Size: 29"
Fork: FOX 36 Float Performance, 140mm, 29"
Shock: FOX Float Performance DPS
Rear Deraileur: SRAM GX Eagle
Shifters: SRAM AXS
Crankset: SRAM Stylo 7k 148 DUB, 32t - 170mm (XS-S), 175mm (M-XL)
Cassete: SRAM XG1275 Eagle 10-50T
Chain: SRAM NX Eagle
Brakes: SRAM Guide R
Brake Rotors: Avid Centerline 180mm 
Headset: Cane Creek 40 IS Integrated Headset
Bars: One Up Carbon riser bar 35mm
Stem: Race Face Aeffect R 50mm
Grips: Santa Cruz Palmdale Grips
Wheels: RESERVE CARBON WHEELS
Front Tire: Maxxis Minion 29x2.3 DHF 3c EXO TR
Rear Tire: Maxxis Minion DHR2 29x2.3 EXO TR
Tubes: Stans Sealant 2oz
Seat Post: Reverb
Saddle: WTB Silverado Pro

active_shortlist.title: 2021 Specialized Stumpjumper Alloy Comp S6 Mountain Bike
active_shortlist.description:
The Stumpjumper Comp Alloy rocks on, with one of the most advanced alloy frames ever developed. From the frame’s asymmetrical design, taking in the refined suspension kinematics and all-new geometry, to a build kit that strikes the optimal combination of performance and value, it’s metal, but it ain’t heavy metal. And it’s ready to slay some dirt.

THE TRAIL BENCHMARK — When we set out to design the all-new Stumpjumper, we pursued some hefty goals. It had to excel on any kind of trail, for every style of rider. To achieve that, we went after serious weight reduction without sacrificing durability, a stiff, responsive chassis that still rides light and lively, geometry that improves everyone’s riding, and the best suspension kinematics we know how to design. Turns out, you can have your cake and eat it too with the new Stumpjumper.

THE SWEETEST 130MM OF TRAVEL YOU’LL EVER RIDE — We massaged the kinematics of the Stumpjumper suspension to bring out the very best all-round performance possible in a 130mm package. Countless hours of suspension lab development and field testing created a refined mid-travel ripper that eats big terrain like a gravity fed beast, handles like a dream everywhere, and climbs like it has a motor.

PROGRESSIVE GEOMETRY — The S-Sizing geometry of the new Stumpjumper hits the progressive sweet spot. It’s long, low and slack, with a short offset fork and a steeper seat tube angle. These numbers combine to make your ride more stable, more carvealicious, more comfortable climbing, more better everywhere. With FlipChip adjustability you’re able to further fine-tune your fit, ensuring that your new Stumpjumper performs flawlessly on whatever terrain you choose.

RIDER-FIRST ENGINEERED™ — Rider-First Engineered™ frames use a size-specific approach to ride characteristics that ensures benchmark handling and flawless performance, regardless of frame or rider size. Whether you’re a jump-happy grom riding an S1, or a DH-fiend giant in need of the lengthy S6, you’ll experience the same ride traits – rail-sharp cornering and balanced front and rear stiffness – at the lightest-possible weight, And by light, we mean real light: 2,250 grams (S-Works, S-3 size, with rear shock).

 S6

OPEN TO OFFERS NO TRADES

active_shortlist.title: Santa Cruz Bronson Mountain Bike
active_shortlist.description:
Selling my Santa Cruz Bronson C. The bike is in great condition with only normal wear and tear from riding. A few small dings and scratches, but no dents or frame damage. It’s been well maintained and cleaned after every ride.

This is full carbon with full suspension and a Fox 36 Performance fork. It’s a solid all around trail bike that climbs well and feels great on descents.

It has New chain, New Maxxis tires, set up tubeless (basically brand new) Minor surface cracking, cosmetic only, RockShox dropper seatpost, New 32mm ODI grips. Frame size: Large
Fits riders roughly 5’9”–6’1” (depending on preference).

The bike is running smooth and ready to ride. Great option if you’re looking for a quality carbon Santa Cruz without paying new prices.

active_shortlist.title: Specialized Enduro Small
active_shortlist.description:
2010 Specialized Enduro size small. Aluminum frame. The only upgrades are SRAM XO gold brakes & rotors, and Tubliss tires.

Everything works as it should, you could ride this bike today.

Pedals are not included, but I could throw in my Crank Bros Mallet pedals for some extra money.

active_shortlist.title: Specialized Full Suspension Mountain Bike
active_shortlist.description:
Selling a high-performance Specialized full suspension MTB in great condition. This is a lightweight carbon fiber trail bike equipped with quality components and built for serious riding.

Details:
	•	Brand: Specialized
	•	Frame: Carbon fiber
	•	Suspension: Full (front and rear)
	•	Rear shock: RockShox Monarch
	•	Fork: RockShox suspension with rebound adjust
	•	Brakes: SRAM Guide hydraulic disc brakes
	•	Dropper post: RockShox
	•	Pedals: Specialized flat platform
	•	Wheels: 29”
	•	Tires: Ground Control / Purgatory (tubeless-ready)

Condition:
	•	Bike rides great
	•	No major damage, just normal cosmetic wear
	•	Lightweight and responsive
	•	Well-maintained

This is a serious trail bike ready for XC, enduro, or all-mountain riding.

summary.buyer_ceiling: $2,500 — the most to pay; $2,500 is the corpus median for the model line across all trims, and the upgraded spec doesn't reliably translate to dollar-for-dollar resale recovery.

summary.logic: The title says "2024 Santa Cruz Tallboy D," but Santa Cruz's published 2024 Tallboy lineup is carbon-only — R, S, X0 AXS, X0 AXS RSV, XX SL AXS RSV, XX AXS RSV. The D alloy trim was the entry-level 2022-and-earlier configuration. Most likely this is a 2022 Tallboy D Aluminum mislabeled as a 2024, which the never-confabulate principle says defaults to PASS or WATCH on its own. Pricing kills it the rest of the way: $2,300 ask is ~82% of the 2022 D's ~$2,800...

summary.logic: At 6 years old, the expected clear for a bike of this class is 35–40% of MSRP (~$1,750–$2,000 against a ~$5,000 comparable complete), yet the seller is asking $3,000 — 60% of MSRP and well above where the depreciation curve puts 6-year-old enduro bikes.
